html:
Stone is a waycastle defending the Eyrie.

The surname Stone is traditionly given to bastard from the Vale of Arryn, such as:

  • Alys Stone
  • Humfrey Stone
  • Mya Stone
  • Orson Stone
  • Robert Stone
  • Samwell Stone
  • Tim Stone

Other usages include:

  • Argoth Stone-Skin, a character in legend.
  • Bloodstone, an island.
  • Bloodstone, a gemstone.
  • Bloodstone Emperor, the last ruler of the Great Empire of the Dawn.
  • Brimstone, a river in southern Dorne.
  • Halder, mockingly known as Stone Head.
  • Harwyn Plumm, known as Hardstone.
  • House Bigglestone, a noble family from the riverlands.
  • House Breakstone, a noble family from the Vale.
  • House Brightstone, an extinct noble family from the Vale.
  • House Stonetree, a noble family from the Iron Islands.
  • House Stonehouse, a noble family from the Iron Islands.
  • Lady Stoneheart, the resurrected Catelyn Stark.
  • Oldstones, a ruined castle in the riverlands.
  • Old Stone Bridge, an inn in the crownlands.
  • Runestone, the seat of House Royce.
  • Shatterstone, the seat of a cadet branch of House Goodbrother.
  • Skagos, means "stone" in the Old Tongue.
  • Stepstones, a chain of islands.
  • Stonedance, the seat of House Massey.
  • Stonedoor, a castle along the Wall.
  • Stonehand, an ironborn raider.
  • Stonesnake, a ranger of the Night's Watch.
  • Stone Cow of Faros, a deity worshipped in Faros.
  • Stone Crows, one of the mountain clans of the Mountains of the Moon.
  • Stone dragon, a prophesied creature.
  • Stone Head, an island in the Summer Islands.
  • Stone Hedge, the seat of House Bracken.
  • Stone men, a term to described the people inflicted by greyscale.
  • Stone Mill, a crossing of the Red Fork.
  • Stone Thumbs, a wildling raider.
  • Stone Way, the official name of the Boneway pass through the Red Mountains.
  • Stoney Sept, a town in the riverlands.
  • Stonyhead, a place in the foothills of the Mountains of the Moon.
  • Stony Shore, a region on the western coast of the north.
  • Toad Stone, an ancient idol in the Basilisk Isles.
  • Willow Pound-Stone, a washerwoman.
